project_goal: Full-stack physics experiment platform: Isaac Sim backend + React web frontend with WebRTC streaming, real-time telemetry, and interactive control.
current_focus: Implement remaining experiments (3-6, 8) — USD physics setup + server telemetry wiring
Keep frontend locked cards for unimplemented experiments
Preserve project continuity across agent sessions

expt1_angular_momentum.notes: Uses teammate USD model + DynamicCuboid proxies

expt2_large_pendulum.notes: Fully functional via senior's WebRTC server code

expt3_ballistic_pendulum.notes: USD asset exists, server handler is placeholder, frontend locked

expt7_momentum.notes: Batch sim.py exists; web server handler is stub — needs USD prim path wiring

how_to_add_experiment: 1. Build USD physics in Experiment/expN/ (rigid bodies, joints, materials)
2. In core/webrtc_server.py: add state vars, _handle_ws_message cases, telemetry in _telemetry_loop
3. In core/webrtc_server.py: update _switch_camera presets if needed
4. In core/webrtc_server.py: add _read_* helpers for telemetry readback
5. In frontend/src/experiments.ts: set isLocked: false, adjust controls/chartConfig
6. (Optional) Add experiments/exptN_name/ for batch analysis with ExperimentBase
operating_rules: Do not depend on prior chat as the only project memory
Record architecture or workflow decisions as ADRs
Write a handoff note when continuity would otherwise be lost
Update this file when project direction or active priorities change
Keep rule files and AGENTS.md versioned in git
open_risks: exp3-6, exp8 USD prim paths in webrtc_server.py are guesses — must match actual USD structure
Experiment runtime behavior needs live Isaac Sim verification
